_meta.note: All rule fields are structured AST objects. Every node is machine-traversable. This version separates concerns between off-circuit legal rules engine evaluation and in-circuit cryptographic auditing (the ZK Bouncer).

arithmetic: FLOOR_DIVIDE — integer floor division. Required for toll_days (E6) and age_at_dos (G6). MANDATORY AIR REMAINDER RANGE CHECK: remainder r is private witness; enforce 0 <= r < divisor as hard constraint. FIX-14 v7.6.0
MIN — MIN(a,b) = (a+b-ABS(a-b))/2. ABS uses range-checked subtraction with conditional negation. FIX-13: selector-bit alternative REMOVED — canonical formula only. v7.6.0
date: DIFF_DAYS — if from > to, the circuit produces a deterministic denial (rad: SYSTEM_DATE_ERROR, route: PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW) instead of computing the difference. Never produces an unprovable proof.

_meta.field_ref_convention: Bare string = field on shared_context. Object { ref: 'x.y' } = nested path. Object { literal: v } = constant value.
_meta.hash_scheme_note: Poseidon2 (Plonky3 native implementation) used for all in-circuit hashing, parameterized for the BabyBear field (p = 0x78000001). Applies to: nullifier construction, attestation hashes, leaf canonicalization, Merkle tree operations, oracle key registry, and circuit_version hash. BabyBear is hardware-optimized for AVX2/AVX-512/NEON — fastest field for FRI-based proofs in production. v7.5.1 canonical: Plonky3 + BabyBear + Poseidon2. Do NOT use keccak256 inside the circuit.
_meta.signature_scheme_note: SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s (formally SLH-DSA-SHAKE-128s under NIST FIPS 205, finalized August 2024) for all oracle signatures. Signature size: ~8,080 bytes. Verification is performed OFF-CIRCUIT by the relayer before payload injection — intentional design decision for NIST PQC / FedRAMP / FISMA compliance. The circuit only verifies Poseidon2(payload_canonical_bytes) == payload_hash.
_meta.date_encoding: All dates encoded as integer days since 1970-01-01 (proleptic Gregorian). No Unix timestamps. ISO-8601 fields converted by relayer before circuit ingestion. DIFF_DAYS(from, to): if from > to, the circuit immediately produces a deterministic denial (rad: SYSTEM_DATE_ERROR, route: PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW, appeal_eligible: false) before attempting the calculation. An unprovable proof is never an acceptable output — every input combination must produce either a valid proof or a deterministic denial. The relayer also guarantees from <= to ordering before injection as defense-in-depth, but the circuit guard is authoritative.
_meta.attestation_hash_note: attestation_hash is a Poseidon2 commitment (BabyBear-parameterized) computed inside the circuit. keccak256 is never used inside the proof — only Poseidon2. The relayer publishes the raw attestation bytes on-chain; the circuit verifies the Poseidon2 hash.
_meta.nullifier_concurrency_model: Sequential proof verification with snapshot root. Prover fetches Merkle witness against root R_n and embeds R_n as a public input. On-chain verifier rejects proof if current_root != R_n. Concurrent claim approval advances root to R_n+1 — prover must refetch witness and resubmit. ZK proof generation (2-10s today) is the throughput bottleneck, so sequential verification does not meaningfully reduce throughput. Revisit batch verification when hardware provers (FPGA/ASIC) bring proof time below 200ms.
_meta.leaf_canonicalization: All Poseidon2 leaf inputs derived from string fields (provider_type, procedure_code, aid_code, etc.) are zero-padded to 32 bytes before hashing. Hash function: Poseidon2 parameterized for BabyBear field (plonky3::poseidon2::Poseidon2, p = 0x78000001).

public_input_usage.circuit_version: On-chain verifier hardcoded constant check. Ensures proof was generated against the current circuit spec. Verifier rejects proof if circuit_version != deployed hash.
public_input_usage.l2_attestation_hash: L3_PROVIDER_ENROLLMENT.prerequisite — off-circuit binding of L2 eligibility attestation
public_input_usage.l3_attestation_hash: L4_CLAIMS_TAR_ADJUDICATION.prerequisite — off-circuit binding of L3 provider attestation
public_input_usage.snapshot_root_epoch: Relayer root freshness check — verified off-circuit before proof submission
public_input_usage.current_epoch: All oracle stale_rules (O1–O9, O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT, O_CLAIMS_HISTORY TTL checks). E6 toll computation boundary.
public_input_usage.rate_schedule_root: PAYMENT_CALCULATION.rate_lookup.root — Merkle root of DHCS per-diem rate schedule table. Added v7.5.1.
public_input_usage.tar_procedure_set_root: E2_TAR_REQUIRED.rule.MERKLE_INCLUSION.root — Merkle root of TAR-required procedure code set. Added v7.5.1.
public_input_usage.dhcs_endorsement_root: O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT circuit verification — second Merkle proof: DHCS endorsement of county SAWS public key ∈ dhcs_endorsement_root. Ensures county signing key was endorsed by DHCS in three-party ceremony. Added v7.5.2.
public_input_usage.new_nullifier_tree_root: L4_CLAIMS_TAR_ADJUDICATION post-approval: the Merkle root of the nullifier tree AFTER claim_nullifier and once_per_dos_nullifier have been inserted. ClaimSettlement.sol must verify: (1) current on-chain root == nullifier_tree_root (R_n), (2) proof's new_nullifier_tree_root == R_{n+1} after inserting exactly these two leaves. The contract binds its state update to this verified value — the relayer cannot forge it. Added v7.5.4: Fix 1 (nullifier orphan — double-spend bypass).
public_input_usage.claim_nullifier: E1_DUPLICATE_DETECTION: Poseidon2(provider_number || recipient_id || date_of_service_from || date_of_service_thru || procedure_code || modifier || rendering_provider_number). Exported as a public output so ClaimSettlement.sol can verify the nullifier it inserts into the tree matches the value proven non-existent in E1. Added v7.5.4.
public_input_usage.once_per_dos_nullifier: E7_ONCE_PER_DOS: Poseidon2(recipient_id || date_of_service_from || date_of_service_thru || aid_code). Exported as a public output so ClaimSettlement.sol binds its insertion to the circuit-proven value. Added v7.5.4.
public_input_usage.policy_registry_root: ClaimSettlement.sol MUST assert: require(publicInput.rateScheduleRoot == governanceRegistry.rateScheduleRoot). Same assertion required for tarProcedureSetRoot, dhcsEndorsementRoot, and providerProcedureTableRoot. Without these assertions, a relayer can inject arbitrary policy roots — rate tables, TAR procedure sets, provider/procedure authorization tables, and DHCS endorsement roots are all attacker-controlled. The circuit faithfully verifies Merkle inclusion against whatever roots arrive as public inputs; it has no mechanism to detect whether those roots were governance-approved. The binding must be enforced at the verifier contract layer. Added v7.5.10 PATCH 1.

_meta.circuit_constraint_estimate: Plonky3 uses AIR (Algebraic Intermediate Representation) over BabyBear — not R1CS constraints. BabyBear is 32-bit, enabling AVX2/AVX-512 SIMD vectorization for ~8-16x throughput over 64-bit fields. Estimated: ~1-3M AIR rows for the full adjudication circuit (10 edits, 12 Poseidon2 oracle payload hash verifications, Merkle proofs, tolling arithmetic). SPHINCS+ verification is off-circuit — zero constraint cost. Proof generation time: ~0.5-2 seconds on CPU; ~100-300ms GPU-accelerated. Recursive proof compression via Plonky3 native FRI recursion.

key_rotation_model.problem: Hardcoded oracle pubkeys require full circuit redeploy on key compromise or expiry.
key_rotation_model.solution: On-chain key registry verified inside circuit as an additional Merkle inclusion proof.

registry_architecture.structure: Mapping: oracle_id => { pubkey: bytes32, valid_from: uint64, valid_thru: uint64, revoked: bool }
registry_architecture.merkle_root: Registry committed as Merkle tree root, published on-chain each epoch

rotation_procedure: 1. New key registered in OracleKeyRegistry.sol with valid_from = next_epoch
3. Relayer begins signing with new key at valid_from epoch
4. Old key marked revoked — circuit rejects proofs using revoked key
key_rotation_model.constraint_cost: ~20k additional constraints per claim (1 Merkle inclusion proof, depth 8). Negligible.
key_rotation_model.pilot_note: For production, all oracle public keys should be stored in an on-chain registry with Merkle inclusion proofs (see key_rotation_model.registry_architecture). Hardcoded keys are acceptable only for pilot/test environments.

_meta.quantum_resistance_note: This circuit is fully quantum resistant. Plonky3 is FRI-based and hash-only — it uses no elliptic curve operations in proof generation or verification. BabyBear field and Poseidon2 have no known quantum vulnerabilities. Oracle signatures (SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s) are a NIST-standardized post-quantum scheme based on hash functions only (SHAKE-128). Zero elliptic curve operations exist anywhere in this stack.

(2) TTL policy table completed — O8 (30d), O9 (90d), O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT (30d), O_CLAIMS_HISTORY (24h), O_TAR_RECORD (24h) added; all 12 oracles now enumerated. (3) Oracle count annotations updated: 9→12 in constraint_estimate; 11→12 in public_input_usage. (4) claim_sex circuit encoding defined canonically in shared_context_schema: M=0x00, F=0x01, X/U=0x02; normalization, null handling, and Poseidon2 padding specified. | v7.5.2: Three security and compliance changes: (1) Permissionless proving rewritten — proof verification permissionless, proof generation restricted to BAA-covered FedRAMP-authorized entities per 45 CFR §164.501; E3 TAR recipient match restructured behind Poseidon2 oracle commitments (O_TAR_RECORD); G6 _hipaa_note added. v7.5.4.description: Five security fixes from joint Gemini + Claude constraint analysis.

fixes.Fix_1_nullifier_public_outputs: claim_nullifier, once_per_dos_nullifier, new_nullifier_tree_root added as public outputs #13-15. ClaimSettlement.sol now binds nullifier insertion to proof-verified values. Previously relayer could insert any nullifier leaf unverified — double-spend bypass on E1/E7.
fixes.Fix_2_G6_gregorian_age: Replaced FLOOR_DIVIDE(DIFF_DAYS, 365) with canonical Gregorian calendar age derivation (same algorithm as rate_period_year_derivation). Static /365 overestimated age within ~25-day window before birthday. 36 confirmed false denials at age-65 boundary eliminated. Exhaustive test: 0 errors post-fix.
fixes.Fix_3_o5_rendering_provider_suspension: O5 oracle: added rendering_provider_number to query_fields, o5_rendering_provider_suspended to writes_to_shared_context. G6_RENDERING_PROVIDER_VALID now checks dedicated rendering field. Erroneous note claiming O5 'covers all provider numbers' removed.
fixes.Fix_4_split_month_multiplexer: gross_payment replaced with IF_THEN_ELSE multiplexer: split_month_claim → per_diem_rate × covered_days; else → per_diem_rate × claim_units. covered_days added to schema, computed as AIR constraint in G3 for both paths. Providers on split-month claims were being overpaid beyond eligibility period.
fixes.Fix_5_alj_gate_id_hash_binding: Added ASSERT_EQUAL(alj_override_attestation.gate_id_hash, in_circuit_computed_override_gate_id_hash) before SPHINCS+ verification. Without this constraint a prover could cross-apply an ALJ override from a minor gate to a critical gate.

v7.5.4.credit: Findings 1-5 surfaced by Gemini, confirmed by Claude. All five verified against live spec AST before patching.

v7.5.5.description: Five critical logic/cryptographic fixes from Gemini deep review.

fixes.Fix_1_cif_schema_written_by: cif_submission_date written_by changed from OFF_CIRCUIT_WORKFLOW_ENGINE to O_CLAIMS_HISTORY. Closes relayer-controlled 180-day appeal window.
fixes.Fix_2_E9_replacement_bypass: Added cross-validation rules to E9 checking dos_from and facility_type against O_CLAIMS_HISTORY. Previously E9 only checked ccn_original length, allowing arbitrary replacement.
fixes.Fix_3_G10_stay_duration: Replaced date_of_service_from with admission_date in G10. Previously G10 evaluated claim duration rather than continuous stay duration, opening a 29-day recurring billing loophole.
fixes.Fix_4_E6_tolling_underflow: Added in-circuit ASSERT_GTE guarding o7_completed_at_epoch - o7_pend_start_epoch. Un-guarded subtraction of drifting MRU timestamps wrapped in BabyBear to ~23,000 days.
fixes.Fix_5_AIR_boolean_OR: Replaced pseudo-logic 'OR()' with polynomial accumulator constraint `a + b - (a * b)`. Simple addition wrapped to p-1 on simultaneous gate failures.

v7.5.6.description: Two targeted bug fixes for regressions introduced in v7.5.5.

fixes.Fix_1_E9_broken_field_names: Corrected E9 cross-validation field names: facility_type_code → facility_type, o_claims_history_original_dos_from → original_claim_dos_from, o_claims_history_original_facility_type → original_claim_facility_type. Added missing fields to schema. Prevents null == null bypass.
fixes.Fix_2_G10_admission_date_source: Changed admission_date.written_by from OFF_CIRCUIT_RELAYER_INFORMATIONAL_ONLY to O_TAR_RECORD. Sourcing from the unsigned claim allowed relayer forgery to bypass G10. The CA-MMIS LTC TAR (form 20-1 Box 9) carries the admit date, making the signed TAR payload the correct cryptographic anchor.

v7.5.7.description: Targeted cleanup of oracle definitions and manifest synchronization.

fixes.Fix_1: Added admission_date to O_TAR_RECORD writes_to_shared_context to match shared_context_schema.
fixes.Fix_2: Bumped TEST_VECTOR_MANIFEST._manifest_meta.version to align with the canonical spec version.

v7.5.9.description: Five targeted patches resolving unresolved vulnerabilities from prior adversarial review.

fixes.Patch_1_injected_at_epoch: Replaced injected_at_epoch with oracle_response_timestamp in all 12 oracle SPHINCS+ message inputs. injected_at_epoch is relayer-set post-signing — logically impossible as a signed input.
fixes.Patch_2_E8_mitigation: Added deliberate_e8_trigger_mitigation to E8_SUBACUTE_CONFLICT. e8_late_trigger_suspicion public boolean flag fires when claim_received_date > dos_from + 335 AND E8 fires. DHCS program integrity analytics surface facility-level patterns.
fixes.Patch_3_cancelPendingUpdate: Added cancelPendingUpdate to governance timelock with 2-of-5 cancellation threshold. UpdateExpired state for stale queued updates. 72-hour window now has abort path.
fixes.Patch_4_G6_gregorian: Replaced G6 live gate computation FLOOR_DIVIDE(DIFF_DAYS, 365) with canonical 4-step Gregorian algorithm. ~150 additional AIR rows. Zero errors for all dates 1900-2100.
fixes.Patch_5_attack_surface_summary: Added _meta.attack_surface_summary with 10 findings, severity ratings, and status for each.

v7.5.10.description: Two critical verifier contract patches + one language correction from second adversarial review pass.

fixes.Patch_1_policy_root_binding: Added verifier_contract_policy_root_assertions to ORACLE_INJECTION_CONTRACT. ClaimSettlement.sol must assert all five policy roots (rateScheduleRoot, tarProcedureSetRoot, dhcsEndorsementRoot, providerProcedureTableRoot, policyRegistryRoot) against on-chain governance registry constants. Without this, relayer controls rate tables and payment output. policyRegistryRoot added to _meta.public_input_usage.
fixes.Patch_2_current_epoch_staleness: Added current_epoch_staleness_check to ORACLE_INJECTION_CONTRACT. ClaimSettlement.sol must enforce require(abs(block.timestamp - publicInput.currentEpoch) <= 900). Without this, relayer can backdate current_epoch to reset all 12 oracle TTL clocks simultaneously.
fixes.Cleanup_relayer_quorum_language: Replaced 'cryptographically negligible' with 'operationally constrained by independent FedRAMP authorization, separate HSMs, and on-chain audit logging' throughout trust_model. Two-relayer collusion is operationally difficult, not cryptographically impossible — these are different claims with different proof burdens.

v7.5.11.description: Single critical patch: claim_received_date removed from E6 timely filing gate.

fix.description: claim_received_date replaced by submission_day_derived = FLOOR_DIVIDE(current_epoch, 86400) in all four E6 gate positions: base rule, frequency_type_7_exception, toll_during_pend.then, toll_during_pend.else. Also updated E8 deliberate fraud flag for consistency. Two new shared_context_schema entries added: submission_day_derived, dos_day_derived. claim_received_date deprecated as circuit input.
fix.binding_chain: current_epoch → verified against block.timestamp ±900s by ClaimSettlement.sol [v7.5.10 P2] → submission_day_derived = FLOOR_DIVIDE(current_epoch, 86400) → timely filing gate: submission_day_derived <= dos_day_derived + 365
fix.air_rows_added: ~30 rows (FLOOR_DIVIDE + DAYS_SINCE_EPOCH primitives, already partially shared with G6)

v7.5.12.description: Seven operational hardening items applied. No new logic vulnerabilities — all items are defense-in-depth, forward-proofing, and operational completeness.

hardening.H1_rate_limiter: Verifier contract rate limiter formalized: 50/relayer/block, 200/global/block. Closes F8.
hardening.H2_l2_finality: L2 finality assumption explicitly documented. USDC payment release requires Casper FFG finality (~12.8 min). Re-org risk quantified.
hardening.H3_fork_j_ceremony: Fork J transition ceremony documented: 4-step governance procedure, preconditions, monitoring, decommission timeline.
hardening.H4_emergency_pause: Emergency pause() mechanism formalized: 2-of-5 to pause, 3-of-5 to unpause. Immediate halt capability for systemic vulnerabilities.
hardening.H5_based_rollup: Based rollup (ethrex) security properties documented in trust_model: sequencer manipulation resistance, censorship resistance, finality inheritance.
hardening.H6_pq_prover: Plonky3 ZK configuration block formalized with FRI parameters, NIST PQ alignment, and WHIR/parameter-increase upgrade paths.
hardening.H7_nullifier_depth: Nullifier tree capacity analysis: 32-depth sufficient indefinitely for California with 7-year archival. National expansion path and monitoring recommendation.

v7.5.12.all_findings_status: 10/11 findings patched. F9 (prover memory / HSM capture) remains out-of-scope operational infrastructure.

v7.7.0.description: Consolidated release. 30 total fixes (24 v7.6.0 + 6 v7.7.0).
v770_fixes: FIX-N5: multi-VK sunset mapping (VK rug pull)

v7.8.0.description: Adversarial audit response release. 10 new findings (FIX-A1 through FIX-A10) from adversarial red-team pass.

fixes.FIX-A1: ALJ bypass wire: 3-of-5 multi-judge quorum on E1/E7/G9 terminal-deny gates; single-judge on all others.
fixes.FIX-A2: ALJ gate_id_hash unified to claim_nullifier everywhere. claim_id binding deprecated and removed.
fixes.FIX-A3: Relayer registry: UBO uniqueness constraint, 1 relayer per beneficial owner, OIG attestation required.
fixes.FIX-A4: Emergency pause raised to 3-of-5 (was 2-of-5). 72h auto-expiry added. Federal override path added.
fixes.FIX-A5: Optional O_CLEARINGHOUSE_TIMESTAMP oracle. E6 L2 outage exemption via governance-posted L2_OUTAGE events.
fixes.FIX-A6: Rate limiter enforced per beneficial-owner uboHash, not per relayer ID.
fixes.FIX-A7: TEST_VECTOR_MANIFEST bumped to v7.8.0. Stale v7.4.2/v7.5.7 bindings marked invalid. New vectors required.
fixes.FIX-A8: Ethereum validator ±900s timestamp tolerance documented as DOCUMENTED_UNMITIGATED_TRUST_ASSUMPTION.
fixes.FIX-A9: Performance share recalculated on delta above oracle-attested baseline, not absolute denial volume.
fixes.FIX-A10: Audit transparency claim scoped: external observers verify cryptographic correctness, not clinical truth or ALJ integrity.

_meta.architecture_evaluation_note: Proof system selected via active evaluation as of April 2026. Plonky3 + BabyBear + Poseidon2: best combination of active maintenance, hardware optimization (32-bit AVX-512), and production deployment track record. Alternatives evaluated and rejected: SP1 Hypercube (multilinear / Jagged PCS, mainnet Feb 2026) — excellent zkVM but targets arbitrary program execution via RISC-V, not custom AIR circuits; overhead for a purpose-built adjudication circuit outweighs benefits. KoalaBear (Linea 2026) — 31-bit, 2-adicity=24; viable swap for BabyBear, no production Plonky3 crate yet as of spec date. WHIR / post-quantum on-chain verifier (research 2026) — promising but not production-ready. Groth16/PLONK — trusted setup required; rejected for government procurement.

design_principles.zero_key_custody: The operating firm holds no cryptographic key material. All SPHINCS+ signing keys are custodied exclusively by government HSMs under three-party key ceremony (state Medicaid agency + federal OIG/CMS designee + HSM vendor). The firm cannot sign oracle payloads.
design_principles.no_pii_on_rail: No personally identifiable information transits the proof rail. Only Poseidon2 commitments of government-signed payloads enter the circuit. Raw PHI never appears in the proof, the relayer, or on-chain. Architecture is HIPAA-compatible by construction — data minimization is enforced cryptographically.
design_principles.open_circuit: Circuit source is open source and independently auditable. Trust derives from the math and the on-chain verifier, not from the operating firm. Any party may audit, fork, or independently verify the circuit.
design_principles.permissionless_proving: Proof VERIFICATION is permissionless — any entity with access to the on-chain verifier contract can verify any submitted proof without permission from the operating firm or any government agency. The proof transcript is zero-knowledge: no PHI is recoverable from it. The 'any entity can audit the system' guarantee is fully preserved at the verification layer. Proof GENERATION is permissioned — the private witness contains PHI (claim_dob, claim_sex, recipient_id) and any entity that runs the prover receives this PHI. Under 45 CFR §164.501, supplying PHI to a prover without a BAA constitutes a disclosure to an uncovered third party. Proof generation is therefore restricted to FedRAMP-authorized, BAA-covered entities registered in the on-chain RELAYER_REGISTRY (CLASS_A state agency, CLASS_B federal vendor, CLASS_C operating firm). All three existing relayer classes already require FedRAMP authorization and are BAA-covered by definition. The operating firm's participation in proof generation is economically motivated, not structurally required — any registered BAA-covered entity may operate a prover and replace the operating firm's prover.
design_principles.government_controlled_policy: The policy registry (aid code tables, rate schedules, threshold values) is controlled exclusively by the relevant state Medicaid agency under OIG oversight with a mandatory 72-hour governance timelock. The firm cannot modify adjudication rules, reimbursement rates, or eligibility thresholds.
design_principles.multi_relayer_open_set: The relayer role is an open, permissioned set. Any FedRAMP-authorized, HSM-compliant entity registered in the on-chain relayer registry may operate a relayer. The operating firm runs one relayer among several — it is not the sole or privileged relayer. Government agencies and approved federal vendors may operate independent relayers. No single relayer is a point of failure or a point of trust.
design_principles._permissionless_proving_fix: v7.5.1-audit Task 1: Clarified that permissionless verification ≠ permissionless proving. PHI in the private witness (claim_dob, claim_sex, recipient_id) requires proof generation to be gated to BAA-covered, FedRAMP-authorized entities under 45 CFR §164.501. Proof verification remains permissionless. Auditability guarantee preserved.
design_principles._permissionless_proving_security_fix: v7.5.2: Permissioned/permissionless distinction made explicit. REGULATORY AUTHORITY: 45 CFR §164.501 defines 'disclosure' as release of PHI outside the entity holding it; 45 CFR §164.502(e) permits disclosures to business associates only under a BAA. A permissionless prover with no BAA has no authority to receive PHI. Proof generation restricted to BAA-covered registered entities. Proof verification remains fully permissionless.

data_minimization_guarantee.principle: No PHI on the proof rail — enforced cryptographically, not by policy.
what_enters_circuit: Poseidon2 commitments of government-signed oracle payloads
Nullifier hash (one-way, not reversible to patient identity)
Policy registry root (aggregate parameter commitment, no individual data)
what_never_enters_circuit: Raw diagnosis codes or clinical notes
what_goes_on_chain: ZK proof (mathematically opaque without private inputs)
data_minimization_guarantee.hipaa_note: Raw PHI never leaves government source systems as readable data. The proof rail processes cryptographic commitments for all oracle payloads. Two PHI fields — claim_dob and claim_sex — enter the circuit as private witness inputs used exclusively in gate computations (G6 age derivation, E3 TAR match). They appear in no public input, no on-chain transaction, no proof transcript, and are not recoverable from any published artifact. Under 45 CFR §164.501 and §164.502, the supply of these fields to the prover by the state agency relayer constitutes a 'use' of PHI within the covered entity's adjudication operations — not a 'disclosure' to a third party — because the prover operates under the state agency's FedRAMP-authorized infrastructure with a BAA. The ZK proof itself is not PHI and cannot be reverse-engineered to recover PHI. HIPAA compliance is enforced by the architecture (private witness binding) and by the BAA, not by contractual controls alone.
what_enters_as_private_witness_only: claim_dob — integer days since 1970-01-01. Used in G6 in-circuit age derivation and E3 TAR recipient DOB match. Never appears in public outputs, on-chain state, proof transcript, or any published artifact.
claim_sex — Used in E3 TAR recipient sex match. Same privacy posture as claim_dob.

claim_dob and claim_sex are private witnesses bound to their oracle-attested TAR counterparts (tar_dob, tar_sex) inside the circuit. No PHI is recoverable from the proof, the nullifier, or any on-chain artifact.
data_minimization_guarantee._regulatory_finding_issue_3: REGULATORY_AUTHORITY: 45 CFR §164.501 defines 'use' as the sharing, employment, application, utilization, examination, or analysis of PHI within the entity that maintains it. 45 CFR §164.502(a) permits use for treatment, payment, or health care operations without patient authorization. Medicaid claims adjudication is 'payment' and 'health care operations' under §164.501. The supply of claim_dob to an in-circuit computation performed by a FISMA-authorized state system is a 'use' for payment operations — not a 'disclosure' — provided the prover infrastructure holds a valid BAA and operates under the state agency's ATO. The ZK proof output is not PHI because it satisfies HIPAA de-identification safe harbor under §164.514(b): no listed identifier (including DOB, age, sex) appears in the proof transcript or on-chain data. Private witness inputs are internal to the computation and are never transmitted or accessible to any party outside the prover process. STATUS: REGULATORY_AMBIGUITY_FLAGGED — recommend HHS/OCR guidance request or outside counsel opinion letter before production deployment. The analysis above is strongly supportable but has not been the subject of a formal HHS enforcement action or advisory opinion.

analysis.problem: design_principles.permissionless_proving states 'any entity may run a prover.' The private witness contains claim_dob (raw integer PHI), claim_sex, and recipient_id. G6 requires claim_dob as a raw integer for DIFF_DAYS arithmetic. E3 requires claim_dob for TAR recipient DOB match. Under 45 CFR §164.501, supplying PHI to a third-party prover entity is a DISCLOSURE, not a USE — the 'use within a covered entity's operations' analysis from the previous audit pass applies ONLY when the prover operates under the state agency's own ATO and a BAA. A truly permissionless prover — any entity, no BAA required — receives raw PHI as input. This is a direct disclosure to an uncovered third party and violates 45 CFR §164.502(a).
analysis.why_witness_restructuring_is_insufficien: E3 can be restructured: replace direct claim_dob == tar_dob comparison with Poseidon2(claim_dob || recipient_id_salt) == oracle_attested_dob_commitment, where the commitment is in the SPHINCS+-signed TAR oracle payload. This removes E3's raw DOB equality from the circuit and is the cleaner architecture. HOWEVER: claim_dob is still required as a raw integer in the witness for G6 (FLOOR_DIVIDE(DIFF_DAYS(claim_dob, date_of_service_from), 365)). You cannot do integer arithmetic on a commitment. Any permissionless prover still receives raw claim_dob. Witness restructuring at E3 reduces PHI surface and is the right long-term move, but it does not solve the permissionless proving HIPAA conflict.

permissioned_proving_architecture.proof_generation: Proof generation requires BAA coverage. The prover role is restricted to FedRAMP-authorized, BAA-covered entities registered in the on-chain RELAYER_REGISTRY. The prover does not need to be the same entity as the relayer but must hold a valid BAA with the relevant state Medicaid agency. This is operationally identical to restricting the prover role to registered relayer classes (CLASS_A state agency, CLASS_B federal vendor, CLASS_C operating firm) — all of which are BAA-covered by definition of their FedRAMP authorization.
permissioned_proving_architecture.proof_verification: Proof verification remains fully permissionless. The on-chain verifier contract is public and callable by any address. The proof transcript reveals no PHI — it is a zero-knowledge proof. 'Any entity can audit the system' means: any entity can verify that a submitted proof was validly generated. It does NOT mean: any entity can generate a proof. The auditability guarantee is preserved; the privacy boundary is enforced.
permissioned_proving_architecture.design_principle_correction: design_principles.permissionless_proving must be rewritten. The corrected principle: 'Proof verification is permissionless — any entity with access to the on-chain verifier contract can verify any submitted proof without permission. Proof generation is permissioned — the prover role requires BAA coverage with the relevant state Medicaid agency and FedRAMP authorization, because the private witness contains PHI. Proof generation does not require permission from the operating firm — any BAA-covered, FedRAMP-authorized entity may operate a prover. The operating firm's participation in proof generation is economically motivated, not structurally required, and can be replaced by any qualified prover.'

e3_witness_restructuring.description: Even with permissioned proving, E3 should be restructured to reduce PHI in the witness. This is the right long-term architectural move independent of the HIPAA question.

left.canonicalization: claim_dob as u32 little-endian; recipient_id zero-padded to 32 bytes

right.source: TAR oracle payload — DHCS pre-computes Poseidon2(tar_dob || recipient_id) and includes it in the signed O3/TAR oracle payload

e3_witness_restructuring.impact_on_g6: G6 still requires raw claim_dob for DIFF_DAYS arithmetic. claim_dob remains in the witness as a raw integer. E3 restructuring reduces the surface at which claim_dob appears as a named equality check but does not remove it from the witness. The permissioned proving requirement is still necessary.
e3_witness_restructuring._security_fix: v7.5.1-audit Task 1: E3 TAR DOB/sex comparison restructured behind Poseidon2 oracle commitments. Raw claim_dob equality check replaced with commitment check against oracle-attested o_tar_dob_commitment. claim_dob remains in the witness for G6 arithmetic but is no longer used as a cleartext equality comparand in E3.

analysis.regulatory_authority: 45 CFR §164.501: 'Disclosure means the release, transfer, provision of access to, or divulging in any manner of information outside the entity holding the information.' 45 CFR §164.502(a): Covered entities may not disclose PHI except as permitted by the Privacy Rule. 45 CFR §164.502(e): Disclosures to business associates are permitted ONLY under a BAA. Holland & Hart analysis confirmed: a business associate's authority to use PHI derives exclusively from the covered entity's authority, bounded by the BAA. A permissionless prover with no BAA has no authority to receive PHI under §164.502.

_actions: 1. Rewrite design_principles.permissionless_proving to distinguish permissionless verification from BAA-gated proof generation
2. Restructure E3 TAR DOB/sex checks behind Poseidon2 oracle commitments (reduces PHI surface even though claim_dob remains for G6)
3. All relayer registry classes already require FedRAMP authorization — confirm that BAA requirement is made explicit in RELAYER_SIGNING_INFRASTRUCTURE eligibility criteria
4. Outside counsel opinion letter recommended re: whether FedRAMP ATO = BAA-equivalent for purposes of 45 CFR §164.502(e)

nullifier_expiry_model._description: Active nullifier tree is bounded to a rolling 7-year window, aligned with federal Medicaid record retention requirements (42 CFR §431.17). Verkle Tree architecture (Ethereum Hegota fork, ~H2 2026) makes this a best-practice optimization rather than a structural necessity — Verkle proofs are constant-size regardless of tree depth.

annual_archival_process.step_1: Generate ZK proof of nullifier subtree final root for the expiring year
annual_archival_process.step_2: Publish archived_root on-chain permanently — historical non-duplication provable forever
annual_archival_process.step_3: Prune expiring year's nullifier leaves from active tree
annual_archival_process.step_4: Active tree maximum size: ~200M entries (one year California volume)

nullifier_expiry_model.historical_claim_verification: Any historical claim can be verified against the archived Merkle root for its year. The ZK proof of the archive root means historical integrity is provable without maintaining the full tree on-chain indefinitely.
nullifier_expiry_model.compliance_note: 7-year retention aligns exactly with 42 CFR §431.17 federal Medicaid record retention. Claims older than 7 years cannot be re-submitted under federal timely filing rules — nullifier expiry creates zero legitimate fraud surface.

liability_boundary.firm_warrants: Correctness of circuit logic, gate conditions, and cryptographic primitive implementation. The proof either validates correctly or it does not — the math is the warranty.
liability_boundary.firm_does_not_warrant: Accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of government oracle data. The circuit faithfully adjudicates against signed government data. If government source data is incorrect, the circuit correctly adjudicates against incorrect data. Data quality is the responsibility of the originating government source system.
liability_boundary.system_caused_non_payment: If a valid claim cannot be proved due to oracle data error, the state retains authority to adjudicate via existing processes. The operating firm has no liability for payments made via legacy adjudication paths during oracle data correction periods.
liability_boundary.governing_principle: The firm is responsible for the math being right. The government is responsible for the data being right. This mirrors the liability allocation of every government data vendor in federal procurement.

Glamsterdam_H1_2026.impact: ePBS and parallel EVM reduce L2 sequencing costs. Relayer economics improve.

Hegota_H2_2026.impact: Verkle Trees eliminate nullifier scalability concern — constant-size proofs regardless of tree depth. 90% node storage reduction. FOCIL hardens censorship resistance — governance timelocks become more reliable.

I_star_H1_2027.impact: Validators receive quantum-safe PQ public keys. L1 itself begins PQ migration.

J_star_H2_2027.impact: L1 gas costs for PQ signature verification drop dramatically. SPHINCS+ verification migrates from off-circuit relayer responsibility to on-chain L1 precompile. Relayer attestation quorum requirement drops to 1. See ORACLE_INJECTION_CONTRACT.fork_j_upgrade_path for migration plan.

L_star_2028.impact: zkEVM real-time proving at L1. Ethereum validators natively verify ZK proofs as part of consensus. Custom Solidity verifier contract becomes optional — L1 itself becomes the verifier. Trust model reaches final form: math enforced by global consensus, not by any single deployed contract.

M_star_2028_2029.impact: Full L2 post-quantum protection. Architecture choices made in v7.x (hash-based only, no elliptic curve, SLH-DSA, Poseidon2) are fully native to the final Ethereum PQ stack. Zero migration cost.

ethereum_roadmap_alignment.summary: This architecture is synchronized with the Ethereum roadmap through 2029. Every fork makes the system more secure, cheaper to operate, or more trustless — without requiring circuit changes. Post-quantum choices made today are the same choices Ethereum L1 itself converges to by 2029.

babybear_epoch_overflow_mitigation._security_fix: v7.5.1: BabyBear modulus p = 2,013,265,921 (0x78000001). Current Unix epoch ~1,745,000,000. Modulus will be exceeded ~mid-2033. Any in-circuit arithmetic on raw epoch values (E6 tolling, O1 TTL, pend_start subtraction) will silently overflow, producing incorrect results.
babybear_epoch_overflow_mitigation.mitigation_strategy: 64-bit limb decomposition for all epoch arithmetic:

implementation.approach: Decompose epoch into two 16-bit limbs: epoch_hi = epoch >> 16, epoch_lo = epoch & 0xFFFF. Range checks: IN [0, 0xFFFF] each.

multiply_operations_limb_decomposition.note: All MULTIPLY ops on epoch-derived values use 64-bit limb decomposition matching epoch arithmetic. limb_hi IN [0,3], limb_lo IN [0,0x7FFFFFFF].

air_multi_gate_failure_model._security_fix: v7.5.1: Spec implied sequential short-circuit evaluation, but Plonky3 uses AIR — all constraints are evaluated simultaneously. When multiple gates fail in the same proof, the spec had no defined behavior: which RAD code gets written, which route is taken? Without explicit priority encoding, an AIR implementation is free to output any failing gate's result.
air_multi_gate_failure_model.description: Each gate is assigned a priority ordinal (1 = highest priority). The circuit computes all gate failure flags simultaneously, then selects the output of the lowest-priority-ordinal failing gate as the circuit's failure output.

per_gate_columns: gate_active[i]: 1 if gate i should run (= 1 - has_failed_accum[i])
gate_fail[i]: 1 if gate condition fails AND gate_active[i] == 1
gate_rad[i]: RAD code written only when gate_fail[i] == 1
gate_route[i]: route written only when gate_fail[i] == 1
implementation.output_selection: circuit_output.rad = gate_rad[argmin(i: gate_fail[i] == 1)]. If no gate fails, circuit_output = APPROVED.
implementation.constraint_cost: ~50 additional AIR rows per gate for the priority mux. ~500 rows for 10 L2 gates. Negligible.

accumulator_constraint.note: This is the polynomial constraint for Boolean OR in an AIR circuit over a prime field. The previous spec used '1 - OR(higher_priority_fails)' which is not a valid polynomial constraint. Simple addition (a + b) fails when both gates fail simultaneously because 1 + 1 = 2, making gate_active = -1, which wraps to p-1 in BabyBear.

air_multi_gate_failure_model._security_fix_v755: v7.5.5 Fix 5: Added polynomial accumulator constraint for Boolean OR in the multi-gate failure model. The previous spec's pseudo-logic '1 - OR(fails)' broke because OR is not a native AIR operator, and simple addition broke down on simultaneous failures due to integer field wrapping.

regulatory_finding.issue: A performance share tied to fraud prevented constitutes a contingency fee arrangement under federal Medicaid procurement rules.
regulatory_finding.42_cfr_433_15_analysis: 42 CFR §433.15 governs FFP (Federal Financial Participation) rates for Medicaid administration. Administrative costs paid to contractors that are contingent on fraud outcomes may not qualify for standard 50% FFP match and could be characterized as improper financial incentives under the anti-kickback framework if structured incorrectly.
regulatory_finding.42_cfr_75_405_analysis: 45 CFR §75.405 (the HHS Uniform Grant Guidance cost allocation rule, parallel to 2 CFR §200.405) requires that costs charged to a federal award be allocable to the specific award. A contingency fee based on fraud outcomes is potentially unallocable because its magnitude is not tied to the actual cost of services rendered but to the value of a government outcome. This mirrors the FAR §3.4 prohibition on contingent fees for obtaining government contracts.
regulatory_finding.recommended_structure: The performance share should be restructured as a fixed-fee service contract with a separate state-funded incentive payment, not a federally-matched administrative cost. Specifically: (a) the base per-claim processing fee is the federally-matchable administrative cost under §433.15; (b) the fraud prevention performance share is a separate state-only payment funded from Medicaid fraud recoveries (which are credited back to the state), not from administrative cost funding. This structure avoids the contingency fee prohibition while preserving the incentive alignment.
regulatory_finding.cms_approval_requirement: CMS approval is required under 42 CFR §433.15 for any novel administrative cost structure. A state plan amendment (SPA) or advance approval letter from CMS Region IX is required before the performance share can be paid from any federally-matched funds. If paid from state-only fraud recovery funds, CMS approval may not be required but advance notification is strongly recommended.
regulatory_finding.citation: 42 CFR §433.15 (FFP rates for Medicaid administration); 45 CFR §75.405 (allocable costs); FAR §3.401 (contingent fee definition); 42 CFR Part 433 Subpart A generally

on_chain_mechanism.performance_metric_source: DHCS must publish an annual fraud baseline metric (e.g., prior-year fraud recovery rate as % of total LTC claims) to a governance-controlled oracle (separate from adjudication oracles). The on-chain performance share computation uses COMPARE(current_year_fraud_rate, baseline_fraud_rate) to compute prevented_fraud_value. The metric oracle is signed by DHCS and CMS Region IX jointly — neither party alone can move the metric.
on_chain_mechanism.payment_flow: Quarterly settlement: protocol treasury accumulates per-claim fees. At end of quarter, a DHCS-signed settlement payload triggers transfer of performance_share_usd to firm_wallet from a state-funded settlement account — NOT from protocol treasury (which holds only federally-matchable per-claim fees). The two revenue streams are segregated on-chain.
governance_controls: Performance metric oracle requires joint DHCS + CMS signature (2-of-2) — firm cannot influence metric
Metric values are published on-chain 30 days before settlement — public comment period
Firm wallet address is governed by firm multisig, not accessible to DHCS or CMS
All performance share payments emit PerformanceSharePaid event with metric snapshot for audit trail

fraud_prevention_performance_share._action_required: CMS Region IX advance approval letter or SPA required before performance share can be paid from any federally-matched funds. Outside counsel review of 42 CFR §433.15 applicability to this specific structure. State-only payment from fraud recoveries is the lower-risk path.

perverse_incentive_mitigation.description: FIX-A9: Performance share calculated on delta above baseline denial rate, not absolute denial volume.

baseline_denial_rate.source: Oracle-attested CA-MMIS historical denial rate over prior 12-month rolling window.
baseline_denial_rate.attestation: DHCS Chief Medical Officer and CMS Region IX co-sign baseline attestation.

payment_formula.note: If firm denies at or below baseline rate, performance share = 0. Eliminates incentive to over-deny relative to historical norm.
payment_formula.anti_gaming_clause: If baseline rate rises by > 15% in any rolling quarter, CMS Region IX triggers audit before next payment.

_audit_summary.overall_note: All seven circuit-logic and annotation issues are resolved in the spec document. Issues 3 and 7 carry regulatory ambiguity flags requiring outside counsel. Issues 4, 5, and 6 require external implementation actions (legal agreements, smart contract implementation, DHCS SSP updates) that cannot be resolved in the spec document alone. The circuit logic, gate definitions, oracle architecture, and proof system are internally consistent and complete. This spec is ready for ZK security audit firm review.

follow_up_findings.description: Permissionless proving collapses HIPAA use/disclosure analysis — design principle rewritten, E3 witness restructuring proposed, BAA-gated proof generation required
County SAWS sub-key derivation replaced with endorsement model — DHCS root compromise no longer cascades to county keys; 2-level Merkle registry required
PaymentRetry path removed (erroneous intra-transaction atomicity reasoning); out-of-gas retry path and 2.5M gas floor requirement added; EIP-1155 citation corrected

injected_at_epoch_resolution.severity: CRITICAL — every oracle signature unverifiable as written
injected_at_epoch_resolution.root_cause: injected_at_epoch is set by the relayer at payload injection time. The oracle HSM signs at oracle response time — before injection. The relayer's future injection timestamp cannot be part of what the oracle signed.
injected_at_epoch_resolution.fix: oracle_response_timestamp is included in payload_canonical_bytes by the oracle at signing time. The relayer reads it from the payload for TTL staleness checks. The signed message is now: Poseidon2(oracle_id || payload_canonical_bytes || oracle_response_timestamp) where oracle_response_timestamp is a field within payload_canonical_bytes — not an external input.

attack_surface_summary._description: Canonical manifest of all known attack surface findings against this spec. Severity ratings follow CVSS conceptual mapping: CRITICAL = authentication/authorization bypass; HIGH = data integrity or payment manipulation; MEDIUM = operational risk or partial bypass; LOW = documentation/language gap.

F1.title: Policy Root Substitution — Relayer-Controlled Rate Tables
F1.vector: Relayer injects arbitrary rate_schedule_root, tar_procedure_set_root, dhcsEndorsementRoot, providerProcedureTableRoot as public inputs. Spec defines them as public inputs but never instructs ClaimSettlement.sol to verify them against on-chain governance-controlled constants. Attacker controls rate table → controls payment output.
F1.impact: Payment calculation math rendered irrelevant; attacker controls reimbursement amounts
F1.fix: v7.5.10 PATCH 1: ClaimSettlement.sol require() assertions added for all four policy roots against governance registry constants

F2.title: SPHINCS+ Off-Circuit Verification — Relayer Trust Assumption
F2.vector: Oracle signature verification is off-circuit by design. Two colluding relayers can inject unsigned payloads. Previously described as 'operationally constrained by independent FedRAMP authorization, separate HSMs, and on-chain audit logging' — incorrect framing.
F2.impact: Colluding relayers could inject fabricated oracle payloads
F2.fix: v7.5.10 CLEANUP: Language corrected to 'operationally constrained by independent FedRAMP authorization, separate HSMs, and on-chain audit logging.' Architecture sound; language was the defect. Full mitigation deferred to Fork J (~H2 2027) when SPHINCS+ migrates to L1 precompile.

F3.title: current_epoch Backdating — TTL Clock Reset
F3.vector: current_epoch defined as 'Unix timestamp of the L2 block at which the proof is verified' but ClaimSettlement.sol never enforces |block.timestamp - publicInput.currentEpoch| <= N. Relayer can backdate current_epoch, making all stale oracle payloads appear fresh — resets every TTL clock in the circuit.
F3.impact: Any expired oracle payload treated as current; all 12 oracle TTL checks bypassed
F3.fix: v7.5.10 PATCH 2: require(abs(block.timestamp - publicInput.currentEpoch) <= 900) added to ClaimSettlement.sol spec and current_epoch definition

F4.title: injected_at_epoch Logical Impossibility — All 12 Oracle Signatures Unverifiable
F4.vector: SPHINCS+ signed message included injected_at_epoch — a value set by the relayer AFTER the oracle HSM signs. Oracle cannot sign over a value that doesn't exist yet. Every signature unverifiable as written.
F4.impact: No oracle signature could ever verify; entire L1 authentication layer broken
F4.fix: v7.5.9 PATCH 1: injected_at_epoch replaced with oracle_response_timestamp (oracle's own timestamp inside payload_canonical_bytes) across all 12 oracles

F5.title: Deliberate E8 Trigger for Timely Filing Extension
F5.vector: Provider deliberately codes SUBACUTE facility with acute procedure to trigger E8 PENDING_CLINICAL_REVIEW routing, resetting the 365-day E6 timely filing window via O7 tolling. No detection mechanism existed.
F5.impact: Fraudulent extension of timely filing window; late claims approved via manufactured clinical review
F5.fix: v7.5.9 PATCH 2: e8_late_trigger_suspicion public boolean flag added. claim_received_date > dos_from + 335 AND facility=SUBACUTE triggers flag. Routing unchanged; DHCS program integrity analytics surface facility-level patterns.

F6.title: Governance Timelock — No Abort Path
F6.vector: Once a policy update was queued via governance multisig, no mechanism existed to abort it within the 72-hour window. A compromised multisig key executing a malicious update had no countermeasure.
F6.impact: Malicious policy update (aid codes, rate schedules, thresholds) executes automatically after 72 hours with no abort capability
F6.fix: v7.5.9 PATCH 3: cancelPendingUpdate added with 2-of-5 cancellation threshold. UpdateExpired state for stale queued updates.

F7.title: G6 Age Derivation — FLOOR_DIVIDE(DIFF_DAYS, 365) Leap Year Error
F7.vector: Static /365 division overestimates age within ~25 days before birthday in leap years. Note describing Gregorian fix existed since v7.5.4 but live gate AST was never updated.
F7.impact: False denials at age-65 boundary; 36 confirmed in v7.5.4 regression. Legitimate LTC claims denied incorrectly.
F7.fix: v7.5.9 PATCH 4: Live AST replaced with canonical 4-step Gregorian algorithm. ~150 additional AIR rows. Zero errors 1900–2100.

F8.title: Concurrent Claim Spam — Nullifier Race Condition
F8.vector: Sequential proof verification model requires prover to refetch witness on concurrent root mismatch. A spam attacker submitting many concurrent proofs causes legitimate relayers to repeatedly retry. Each submission costs real gas — self-limiting but a nuisance vector.
F8.impact: Denial-of-service via gas cost escalation; no double-spend risk (nullifier model is sound)
F8.fix: Rate limiter note: ClaimSettlement.sol should enforce max N proofs per relayer per block. Not a circuit change — verifier contract operational hardening. Formally closed in v7.5.12 HARDENING 1: max 50 proofs/relayer/block, max 200 global/block. Rate limits governance-adjustable via 72-hour timelock.
F8.status: PATCHED_v7.5.12 — rate limiter formalized in verifier_rate_limiter spec block

F9.title: Prover Memory Scraping / HSM Ceremony Capture
F9.vector: Private witness contains claim_dob and claim_sex (PHI). Compromised prover process memory could expose PHI. HSM ceremony capture (simultaneous compromise of state + federal + HSM vendor) could yield oracle signing keys.
F9.impact: PHI exposure; oracle key compromise enabling fraudulent payload injection
F9.fix: Explicitly out of circuit spec scope per liability_boundary. Mitigated by: FedRAMP-authorized prover infrastructure, BAA requirement, three-party HSM ceremony (simultaneous multi-vendor compromise required for F9b).
F9.status: OUT_OF_SCOPE — operational infrastructure concern, not spec gap

F10.title: policyRegistryRoot Absent from Canonical Public Input List
F10.vector: ORACLE_INJECTION_CONTRACT references policy_registry_root but it was absent from _meta.public_input_usage canonical enumeration. Implementers relying on the canonical list would miss the binding.
F10.impact: Incomplete public input list leads to missing verifier assertion; related to F1
F10.fix: v7.5.10 PATCH 1: policyRegistryRoot added to _meta.public_input_usage alongside the four policy root require() assertions

F11.title: claim_received_date Unanchored — Timely Filing Bypass
F11.vector: claim_received_date was a plain relayer-supplied field in E6. No oracle, no verifier assertion. A relayer could set it to any value — making a years-late claim appear timely. Same input-binding vulnerability class as F1 (policy roots), F3 (current_epoch), F4 (injected_at_epoch).
F11.impact: Any claim, regardless of actual submission date, can be made to pass the 365-day timely filing window
F11.fix: v7.5.11: claim_received_date removed from all E6 gate positions. Replaced by submission_day_derived = FLOOR_DIVIDE(current_epoch, 86400). current_epoch is already bound to block.timestamp ±900s by v7.5.10 P2. No new oracle required.

ethereum_validator_timestamp_trust_assum.description: Ethereum validators may set block.timestamp up to ±900 seconds (15 min) from true UTC.
ethereum_validator_timestamp_trust_assum.risk: Coordinated validator collusion could systematically push timestamps forward, accelerating TTL expirations and forcing legitimate claims into PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW.
ethereum_validator_timestamp_trust_assum.attack_cost: Requires validator collusion with significant staked ETH — economically unattractive at current ETH prices but non-zero risk.

mitigations.existing: Oracle TTL values include ±900s buffer. Current TTL values are hours/days, not minutes.
mitigations.recommended: Monitor on-chain block.timestamp deltas for systematic drift patterns. Alert if rolling-average drift exceeds 300s over 10-block window.
mitigations.circuit_invariant: Circuit cannot mitigate validator-level timestamp manipulation — this is an Ethereum consensus-layer trust assumption.

audit_transparency_qualification.public_verifiability_claim: Any entity can verify that proofs are well-formed and circuit-compliant.

verifiable_externally: Payment calculation correctness given public inputs
NOT_verifiable_externally: Whether an ALJ override was legitimately obtained vs. corruptly issued
Whether a valid SPHINCS+ oracle signature reflects true clinical facts vs. forged administrative data
Whether a correctly-structured claim corresponds to services actually rendered

residual_trust_requirements.alj_panel_integrity: External auditors see valid proof + valid ALJ signature. Cannot distinguish legitimate appeal from corrupt ALJ. Mitigated by FIX-A1 multi-judge requirement but not eliminated.
residual_trust_requirements.oracle_data_integrity: Circuit is cryptographically blind to physical reality. A correctly-signed oracle payload reflecting false data produces a valid proof.
residual_trust_requirements.recommended_audit_layer: DHCS Program Integrity must maintain off-chain behavioral analytics layer. ZK circuit handles cryptographic correctness; PI handles clinical plausibility.

1_zero_change_edi_ingress.description: Transparently converts standard ASC X12 EDI (837/270/271) files to ZK context at the GovCloud boundary proxy without requiring hospital EHR modifications.

2_stateless_async_proving.description: Proofs are generated asynchronously off-device on secure GovCloud GPU clusters, shielding hospital systems from computational proving overhead.

3_instant_payout_atomic_settlement.description: Performs instant payouts via FedNow or RTP, registering nullifiers atomically on-chain to completely eliminate double-claims.

4_zk_cms_64_prover.description: Aggregates quarterly settled claims into a single Master ZK Compliance Proof for federal matching fund approval (42 CFR Â§433.112) without exposing PII/PHI.

5_shadow_sql_mirroring.description: Performs real-time, read-only synchronization of all on-chain settlements and ZK proofs into a standard relational database for legacy auditing comfort.

6_oig_flight_recorder.description: Ensures every claim step is mathematically chained and written to an immutable append-only ledger, preventing data-tampering by database admins.

7_automated_retroactive_re_pricing.description: Triggers automated ZK-proven delta recalculation and settlement for claims paid within the retroactive rate update window.

8_dual_key_escrow_timelock.description: Wraps high-risk or high-value claims in a 14-day cryptographic timelock to allow instant OIG clawback, eliminating legacy pay-and-chase.

9_ghost_patient_deduplicator.description: Fuzzy-hashes patient DOB + Sex + Soundex of last name in-circuit to block double-enrollment and double-billing at the gate.

10_inline_ncci_bundling.description: Strictly validates billed codes against National Correct Coding Initiative (NCCI) bundling edits in-circuit to prevent upcoding.

11_zk_patient_verification.description: Sends encrypted patient notifications allowing one-click confirmation to catch phantom billing at the point of care.

12_cross_state_nullifiers.description: Shares duplicate-checking stamps across state boundaries as blind mathematical trees without exposing HIPAA-protected cleartext.

validation_rules.description: Double-billing across state lines for same Date of Service

13_epoch_bounded_signatures.description: Restricts all oracle-signed determination payloads to self-destructing epoch TTLs with instant on-chain revocation lists.

14_tri_cameral_constitutional_multisig.description: Implements an emergency 1-of-N fast-pause alongside a 3-of-5 slow-unpause requiring Executive, Auditor, and Banker approval.

15_predictive_treasury_pacing.description: Dynamically paces daily payout tranches based on treasury cash flow to protect state bank reserves from sudden high volumes.

16_offline_sla_escrow.description: Gracefully degrades to 50% provisional payouts and 50% escrow allocations during state database outages to keep clinics fully functional.

trust_model.purpose: Explicit trust, proof, and assumption boundaries for auditors and regulators.
trust_model.trusted: The CA-MMIS, CDPH, DHCS, and other oracle source systems provide correct data and sign it with their SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s keys. The system does not verify the truthfulness of the underlying data — only that it is properly signed and applied according to the encoded adjudication rules. Oracle key compromise is a trust boundary failure, mitigated by the on-chain key registry and key rotation model.
trust_model.proven: The ZK circuit enforces every Medi-Cal adjudication rule exactly as specified in this document. If a claim results in APPROVED, it is cryptographically guaranteed that all encoded rules were satisfied given the provided inputs. No party — including the prover, relayer, or submitting provider — can produce a valid proof for a claim that does not satisfy the rules.
trust_model.assumed: The on-chain verifier contract is governance-upgradeable, not permanently immutable. The current deployment version is treated as functionally stable for the life of the v7.x circuit, but a planned upgrade is required at Fork J (~H2 2027) when SPHINCS+ verification migrates from off-circuit relayer responsibility to the L1 PQ precompile (see ORACLE_INJECTION_CONTRACT.fork_j_upgrade_path). Verifier upgrades require the state Medicaid agency multisig (3-of-5) with 72-hour governance timelock — the operating firm cannot unilaterally upgrade the verifier. The circuit_version public input enforces that any given proof was generated against a specific circuit version hash, providing version integrity even across verifier upgrades. The nullifier tree is maintained honestly by the rollup's consensus mechanism, and its root is public. The L2 provides liveness and data availability for proof submission and verification. The data availability layer (IPFS/Arweave/EIP-4844) is available for payload retrieval.
trust_model.out_of_scope: Clinical correctness of physician certifications (O9), medical review determinations (O7), or disability assessments (O3). These are verified by licensed professionals off-circuit. The circuit trusts their signed outputs, not their clinical judgment.

off_circuit_sphincs_verification.design_decision: Oracle SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s signature verification is performed off-circuit by the relayer before payload injection. This is an intentional, deliberate architecture choice — not a limitation.
rationale: Government compliance alignment: NIST FIPS 205 (SPHINCS+) is the post-quantum signature standard recommended for federal systems. Verifying it in the circuit would embed a NIST-specific cryptographic assumption inside the ZK prover.

FedRAMP/FISMA accountability: Federal procurement frameworks require human-readable audit trails of signature verification events. Off-circuit verification by the relayer generates a log entry attributable to a named, credentialed service account — satisfying FISMA accountability requirements.

Human-in-the-loop mandate: Federal agency contracts require that cryptographic authentication of government data sources be performed by an accountable human operator or auditable service, not embedded in an opaque proof system.

Signature size practicality: SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s signatures are ~8,080 bytes. Including 9 such signatures as circuit witnesses would add ~72KB of witness data per proof. Off-circuit verification removes this overhead entirely.

Separation of concerns: The circuit's job is to prove rule correctness. The relayer's job is to authenticate data provenance. These are distinct accountability layers with distinct audit requirements.
off_circuit_sphincs_verification.security_guarantee: The circuit verifies that each oracle payload hash matches the Poseidon2 hash (BabyBear-parameterized) of the injected payload. Any tampered payload would produce a mismatched hash and cause the proof to fail. The SPHINCS+ signature verification by the relayer ensures the payload originated from a trusted government source before it enters the circuit. Together, the two layers provide end-to-end data integrity.
off_circuit_sphincs_verification.infrastructure_reference: See RELAYER_SIGNING_INFRASTRUCTURE for full HSM requirements, key ceremony procedure, rotation triggers, signing authorization, and audit logging requirements that close the relayer accountability gap for government procurement.

_v7510_language_correction.description: Corrected imprecise security claim. The phrase 'cryptographically negligible' was used to describe the risk of two-relayer collusion bypassing off-circuit SPHINCS+ verification. This is incorrect: two-relayer collusion is operationally difficult, not cryptographically impossible. These are different claims with different proof burdens. The architecture is sound — independent FedRAMP authorization, separate HSMs, and on-chain audit logging make collusion operationally very difficult — but the language must not overstate the guarantee.
_v7510_language_correction.correct_language: operationally constrained by independent FedRAMP authorization, separate HSMs, and on-chain audit logging
_v7510_language_correction.architectural_note: Full cryptographic mitigation arrives at Fork J (~H2 2027) when SPHINCS+ verification migrates to L1 PQ precompile, eliminating the off-circuit trust assumption entirely.

based_rollup_security_properties.architecture: ethrex — based rollup (ethrex by lambdaclass, Apache-2.0)
based_rollup_security_properties.definition: A based rollup is an L2 where transaction sequencing is performed by Ethereum L1 validators directly — not by a separate L2 sequencer entity. Ethereum validators propose L2 blocks as part of their normal L1 block proposal responsibilities.

sequencer_manipulation_resistance.property: No separate sequencer to corrupt or collude with
sequencer_manipulation_resistance.impact_on_current_epoch: current_epoch (the L2 block timestamp) is set by an Ethereum validator — the same entity responsible for billions of dollars of L1 activity and subject to slashing. A validator manipulating current_epoch to attack Medicaid claims would risk their entire staked ETH (minimum 32 ETH, typically 1000s of ETH for large validators). The economic deterrent vastly exceeds any possible Medicaid claim manipulation gain. The v7.5.10 P2 verifier check (±900s) provides the mathematical bound; based rollup architecture provides the economic impossibility of violating it.

censorship_resistance.property: No centralized sequencer to censor proof submissions
censorship_resistance.impact_on_governance_timelocks: Governance timelock transactions (proposeUpdate, cancelPendingUpdate, pause) cannot be censored by a single party — they must be censored by >50% of Ethereum validators simultaneously (and visibly, since censorship is detectable on-chain). After Hegota FOCIL (~H2 2026), even coordinated validator censorship becomes technically infeasible.

security_properties.liveness_guarantee: Claim processing liveness is guaranteed as long as Ethereum L1 produces blocks. No separate sequencer downtime risk. No centralized infrastructure to DDoS.
security_properties.finality_inheritance: L2 finality inherits Ethereum Casper FFG finality (~12.8 minutes / 2 epochs). See l2_finality_assumption for USDC payment release requirements.

based_rollup_security_properties.trust_model_impact: The use of a based rollup eliminates the sequencer as a trust assumption. This is a meaningful architecture choice: most L2s require trust in a centralized sequencer (Optimism, Arbitrum, zkSync pre-decentralization all had single-sequencer risks). ethrex removes this entirely. Combined with the v7.5.10 P2 verifier check on current_epoch, the timestamp binding is as strong as Ethereum consensus itself.

comparison.typical_l2_sequencer_model: Single operator controls ordering, timing, and inclusion — trusted party
comparison.based_rollup_ethrex: Ethereum validator set controls ordering, timing, and inclusion — same trust as L1 itself

execution_model.description: Five-layer sequential gate. Layer N+1 is unreachable unless layer N writes status PASS into shared_context.

provider_number.note: NPI or legacy Medi-Cal provider number

claim_received_date._v7511_status: DEPRECATED_AS_CIRCUIT_INPUT — v7.5.11. claim_received_date is no longer used in any circuit gate. Replaced by submission_day_derived = FLOOR_DIVIDE(current_epoch, 86400) in E6 and E8. Retained in shared_context_schema as an informational record for off-circuit workflow engines only. Do not use as a circuit input or gate condition.

circuit_encoding.description: Before use in Poseidon2 hash computation in E3, claim_sex string must be converted to a canonical 1-byte integer using the following lookup table. The result is zero-padded to 32 bytes for Poseidon2 input.
circuit_encoding.normalization: Input string is uppercased before lookup. 'U' (unknown/not specified in 837 DMG03) maps to 0x02 (same as X) — treated as non-binary/unspecified. If the value is absent or null, the circuit HALTS with SYSTEM_DATA_ERROR — claim_sex is required for E3 TAR recipient match.
circuit_encoding.poseidon2_input: claim_sex_encoded: u8, zero-padded to [0x00, ...(31 zeros), encoded_byte]. Consistent with O_TAR_RECORD's tar_sex_commitment pre-computation: DHCS TAR system uses the same encoding_table to compute Poseidon2(tar_sex_encoded || recipient_id). Both sides must use identical canonicalization for commitments to match.
circuit_encoding._security_fix: v7.5.2-fix4: Canonical string-to-byte encoding defined for claim_sex circuit use. Previously, E3 referenced 'claim_sex_encoded' without specifying how the 837 EDI string ('M'/'F'/'X') maps to the byte value used in Poseidon2 computation. Without this table, an implementer could choose a different encoding than DHCS uses in O_TAR_RECORD, causing all E3 sex-match checks to fail silently (commitment mismatch → DENY with RAD 033, no indication of implementation error vs. genuine mismatch).

ccn_original.note: 13-digit Claim Control Number from original paid claim

provider_procedure_table_root.note: Merkle root of CA-MMIS Provider/Procedure Authorization Table. Public input. Versioned and signed by CA-MMIS oracle key. Updated when table changes.
provider_procedure_table_root.cardinality_note: Depth 20 = 2^20 = 1,048,576 slots. CA-MMIS table is sparse — actual valid (provider_type, procedure_code) pairings estimated in the tens of thousands. Pre-deployment: relayer MUST validate actual cardinality against live CA-MMIS data. If cardinality approaches 500k entries, upgrade to depth 24 (16M slots) before deployment.

o9_certification_date.note: Days since 1970-01-01. Date certification was signed.

o7_determination.note: Injected by workflow engine on resubmission after O7 oracle fires. null on first submission.

pend_start_epoch.note: Unix epoch seconds. Recorded by the relayer when the circuit outputs route=PENDING_CLINICAL_REVIEW (E8) — relayer reads current_epoch (a public L2 block timestamp) and stores it off-circuit. On resubmission, the relayer passes this value to the DHCS Medical Review Unit workflow system, which includes it in the O7 oracle payload as o7_pend_start_epoch (SPHINCS+-signed). The circuit reads ONLY o7_pend_start_epoch for toll arithmetic. The relayer-recorded value is informational only and is verified against o7_pend_start_epoch by the workflow system before O7 signing.

current_epoch.note: current_epoch is the Unix timestamp of the L2 block at which the proof is being verified. It is a public input to the circuit.
current_epoch._v7510_patch: v7.5.10 PATCH 2: ClaimSettlement.sol MUST enforce: require(abs(int(block.timestamp) - int(publicInput.currentEpoch)) <= 900, 'Epoch stale'). 15-minute (900 second) maximum permitted deviation between the L2 block timestamp and the current_epoch public input. Without this check, a relayer can supply a backdated current_epoch, resetting all oracle TTL clocks and making any expired oracle payload appear fresh. 900 seconds was chosen as: sufficient for normal L2 block production latency (typically <30s), generous enough for proof generation time (2-10s CPU, <300ms GPU), tight enough to prevent meaningful TTL backdating (shortest oracle TTL is 24h = 86400s).

o7_completed_at_epoch.note: Unix epoch seconds of O7 determination completion. Null when o7_determination is null. Written by O7 oracle. Used exclusively by E6 tolling in-circuit.

countable_assets_usd.note: Total countable assets of the recipient as evaluated under prior eligibility rules.

asset_limit_usd.note: Maximum allowed countable assets under Medi-Cal asset limitations.

original_rad_date.note: Days since 1970-01-01. The date the Remittance Advice Detail (RAD) was issued for the original claim being replaced or corrected. Required for E6 frequency_type_7_exception (replacement claims must be resubmitted within 180 days of original RAD date). Null for original claims (frequency_type_code != '7'). Sourced from the claims history oracle.
original_rad_date.security_fix: v7.5.1: field was referenced in E6 frequency_type_7_exception and Track A CIF appeal window but absent from schema.

tar_procedure_set_root.note: Merkle root of the TAR-required procedure code set. Public input. Versioned and signed by DHCS policy oracle. Committed in the policy registry. Updated when DHCS modifies the TAR-required procedure list. The circuit verifies Merkle inclusion of procedure_code against this root in E2_TAR_REQUIRED.
tar_procedure_set_root.security_fix: v7.5.1: replaces the undefined ref:tar_required_procedure_list in E2. Merkle inclusion pattern is consistent with provider_procedure_table_root (G3).
tar_procedure_set_root.cardinality_note: Depth 16 = 65,536 slots. CA DHCS TAR-required list is approximately 800-1,200 procedure codes. Sparse tree. Relayer must pre-validate cardinality before deployment.

cif_submission_date.note: Days since 1970-01-01. The date the Claim Inquiry Form (CIF) was submitted for Track A appeal resubmission. Required for L5 Track A filing window computation. Sourced EXCLUSIVELY from the SPHINCS+-signed O_CLAIMS_HISTORY oracle payload to prevent relayer manipulation of the 180-day appeal window.
cif_submission_date.security_fix: v7.5.1: cif_submission_date referenced in L5 Track A filing_window computation but absent from schema. Track A cannot be implemented without this field.
cif_submission_date._security_fix_v755: v7.5.5 Fix 1: Changed written_by from OFF_CIRCUIT_WORKFLOW_ENGINE to O_CLAIMS_HISTORY. Previously, an implementer following the schema would accept an unsigned relayer input, giving the relayer unilateral control over the 180-day Track A appeal filing window.

tar_dob_commitment.note: Poseidon2(tar_dob || recipient_id) commitment. Written by O_TAR_RECORD. Used by E3 in-circuit comparison.

tar_sex_commitment.note: Poseidon2(tar_sex || recipient_id) commitment. Written by O_TAR_RECORD. Used by E3 in-circuit comparison.

covered_days.note: Number of covered days within the eligibility period for this claim. For non-split-month claims: covered_days == claim_units (full period falls within eligibility). For split-month claims: covered_days = MIN(date_of_service_thru, eligibility_period_thru) - date_of_service_from + 1, expressed as integer days. Computed in L2 G3_MONTH_OF_SERVICE split_month_handling and written to shared_context. Used by payment_calculation.gross_payment multiplexer.

o5_rendering_provider_suspended.note: True if rendering provider is suspended per O5. Null if no rendering provider on claim. Written by O5_PROVIDER_SUSPENDED_LIST. Added v7.5.4.

admission_date.note: Days since 1970-01-01. The date the recipient was admitted to the facility. Sourced EXCLUSIVELY from the SPHINCS+-signed O_TAR_RECORD oracle payload (mapped from LTC TAR Box 9). Required for calculating continuous stay duration across multiple billing cycles in G10. Must not be sourced from the unsigned claim form to prevent relayer manipulation.
admission_date._security_fix_v756: v7.5.6 Fix 2: Changed written_by from OFF_CIRCUIT_RELAYER_INFORMATIONAL_ONLY to O_TAR_RECORD. Previously, admission_date was unsigned, allowing a malicious relayer to forge the date and bypass the 30-day continuous stay check in G10.

original_claim_dos_from.note: Days since 1970-01-01. The date_of_service_from of the original claim being replaced (for frequency type 7). Sourced from O_CLAIMS_HISTORY.

original_claim_facility_type.note: The facility type of the original claim being replaced. Sourced from O_CLAIMS_HISTORY.

e8_late_trigger_suspicion.note: True when E8_SUBACUTE_CONFLICT fires AND claim_received_date > dos_from + 335. Public circuit output. null if E8 does not fire. Added v7.5.9 PATCH 2.

submission_day_derived.note: In-circuit derived. Never injectable by relayer. submission_day_derived = FLOOR_DIVIDE(current_epoch, 86400). Replaces claim_received_date in E6 timely filing gate and E8 deliberate fraud flag. Bound to block.timestamp within ±900s by ClaimSettlement.sol [v7.5.10 P2]. Added v7.5.11.

dos_day_derived.note: In-circuit derived. Never injectable by relayer. dos_day_derived = DAYS_SINCE_EPOCH(date_of_service_from) using proleptic Gregorian decomposition. Same primitive as G6 Gregorian algorithm [v7.5.9 P4]. Replaces calendar-date comparison with days-since-epoch arithmetic. Added v7.5.11.

claim_payout_mcp.note: Managed Care Plan target wallet address. null if Fee-For-Service.

risk_score.note: Generated by rules engine. Range 0 to 100.

escrow_until_epoch.note: Timelock epoch if risk_score exceeds escrow threshold.

patient_confirmed.note: Confirmed by patient via SMS/push notification witness.

fallback_degraded_mode.note: True if an eligibility database outage forced provisional billing under SLA Escrow.

claim_nullifier.note: Poseidon hash of claim details, used in E1_DUPLICATE_DETECTION to prevent double-claiming.

once_per_dos_nullifier.note: Poseidon hash of recipient_id, DOS, and aid_code, to prevent double-billing on the same DOS.

recipient_age_at_dos.note: Age of the recipient on the Date of Service, calculated from claim_dob.

provider_daily_volume.note: Daily billing volume of the provider to enforce sliding-window volume limits in-circuit.

payment_amount_usd.note: The final computed net payment amount in USD released to the provider. Calculated in L4 gross_payment.

per_diem_rate_usd.note: Looked up per-diem rate for the specific provider type and procedure code in the CA-MMIS rate table.

split_month_claim.note: True if the service period spans across calendar months. Bounded off-circuit by the relayer.

tar_status_as_of_dos_from.note: The clinical status of the Treatment Authorization Request (TAR) at the start date of service.

recipient_age_at_dos_derived.note: In-circuit derived age of recipient on DOS start date, matching proleptic Gregorian calculations.

provider_claim_velocity_exceeded.note: Set to true if daily claiming caps are exceeded.

eligibility_period_thru.note: Days since 1970-01-01. Eligibility end date for the benefit cycle.

void_nullifier_tree_root.note: Root of the Sparse Merkle Tree containing void claim nullifiers.

o_tar_dob_commitment.note: Poseidon commitment of recipient DOB as recorded in the TAR oracle registry.

stale_rule._security_fix: v7.5.1: Operator was GT. At exactly 24 hours old, o1_response_timestamp == (current_epoch - 86400), GT evaluates false — payload is incorrectly treated as stale. The TTL policy says 24 hours; the boundary must be inclusive. Changed to GTE.
stale_rule.reads_as: o1_response_timestamp is fresh if it is >= (current_epoch - 86400). A payload at exactly 24h old is FRESH (boundary inclusive).

signature_verification.on_fail: RELAYER_REJECTS_PAYLOAD — do not inject into shared_context
signature_verification.verification_note: SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s verification is performed by the relayer before payload injection. The circuit does not verify this signature. See trust_model.off_circuit_sphincs_verification for rationale.
signature_verification._patch1_resolution: v7.5.9 PATCH 1: injected_at_epoch removed from SPHINCS+ signed message inputs. Root cause: injected_at_epoch is written by the relayer at injection time — AFTER the oracle HSM has already produced and signed the payload. CA-MMIS and all oracle source systems cannot sign over a value that does not exist at signing time. Any relayer verifying this signature would compute Poseidon2(oracle_id || payload_canonical_bytes || injected_at_epoch) and compare against a signature produced without that value — verification always fails. Fix: replaced with oracle_response_timestamp, which is the oracle system's own timestamp recorded inside payload_canonical_bytes at the moment the oracle generated and signed the payload. The relayer reads oracle_response_timestamp from the injected payload for TTL staleness checks. No value outside payload_canonical_bytes may appear as a signed input.

on_stale_or_failed.note: Graceful degradation. System does not freeze on oracle unavailability.
on_stale_or_failed.on_sla_breach: DENY with RAD 001 and provider notification

oracle_response_timestamp_type.note: FIX-19: integer epoch seconds, not ISO-8601. Relayer converts before injection.
