PASS_CORRECT_VERSION.description: Proof generated with circuit_version public input matching v7.5.1 canonical hash. On-chain verifier must accept the proof.

claim.note: Any well-formed APPROVED claim — this test exercises the verifier binding, not adjudication logic.

PASS_CORRECT_VERSION.rationale: The on-chain Solidity verifier has the v7.5.1 circuit_version hash hardcoded. A proof carrying the matching public input passes the version gate and proceeds to full proof verification.

FAIL_MISMATCHED_VERSION.description: Proof generated with circuit_version public input from a prior spec version (v7.3.3). On-chain verifier must reject the proof — the circuit fingerprint does not match the deployed verifier's hardcoded constant.
FAIL_MISMATCHED_VERSION.circuit_version_input: Poseidon(b'7.3.3' [pre-v7.4.1 used Poseidon; v7.4.1+ uses Poseidon2] zero-padded to 32 bytes)

claim.note: Same well-formed claim as CVB_PASS_001 — only circuit_version differs.

FAIL_MISMATCHED_VERSION.rationale: This test proves the on-chain verifier correctly rejects proofs generated by outdated circuit instances. An operator cannot silently substitute an older, potentially less restrictive circuit — the version binding is enforced at the smart contract level, not just by convention.
FAIL_MISMATCHED_VERSION.security_note: This adversarial vector is critical for the security model. Without version binding, a malicious prover could use a circuit compiled from a prior spec (e.g., one with a known weakness or a missing gate) and submit proofs to the current verifier. The version hash prevents this class of downgrade attack.

TEST_VECTOR_MANIFEST._note: All vectors valid against v7.5.1. G6 vectors updated: claim no longer carries recipient_age_at_dos — age is derived in-circuit from claim_dob and date_of_service_from.

_section_meta.purpose: Specifies the infrastructure requirements for the relayer component responsible for off-circuit SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s oracle signature verification. This section closes the relayer accountability gap for government procurement, FedRAMP authorization, and FISMA compliance. Referenced by: trust_model.off_circuit_sphincs_verification.
compliance_frameworks: FedRAMP Rev5 High baseline (SC-12, SC-17, IA-3, AU-2, AU-9, AU-11)
_section_meta.accountability_owner: Relayer Operator — named, credentialed service account with HSM access
_section_meta.sphincs_formal_name: SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s is formally standardized as SLH-DSA-SHAKE-128s under NIST FIPS 205 (finalized August 12, 2024). Both names refer to the same algorithm. Use 'SLH-DSA-SHAKE-128s' in government procurement documents and FedRAMP SSP; use 'SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s' in technical implementation documents.

HSM_REQUIREMENTS.rationale: FIPS 140-2 Level 3 requires physical tamper-evidence and tamper-response mechanisms. Private keys never exist in plaintext outside the HSM boundary. Level 2 is acceptable for commercial deployments; Level 3 is required for any federal agency deployment or FedRAMP-authorized production environment.
HSM_REQUIREMENTS.key_material_constraint: SPHINCS+ private key material must never exist in plaintext outside the HSM at any point in its lifecycle — generation, storage, use, rotation, and destruction.
HSM_REQUIREMENTS.signing_api: HSM exposes PKCS#11 or vendor-native API. The relayer calls Sign(payload_hash) — the raw key bytes are never returned.
HSM_REQUIREMENTS.disaster_recovery: HSM key backup must use HSM-to-HSM encrypted transfer only. No plaintext key export under any circumstances.

procedure: Step 1 — Air-gapped ceremony room. No network connectivity. Faraday cage recommended for high-security deployments.
Step 2 — Each participant holds one key-ceremony smart card (hardware token). Quorum of 2 of 3 required to initialize the HSM.
Step 3 — HSM generates SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s key pair internally. Private key never leaves HSM boundary.
Step 4 — Public key is exported and committed to the on-chain oracle_key_registry via governance transaction.
Step 5 — Ceremony is video-recorded. Attendance log signed by all participants and preserved for audit.
Step 6 — Smart cards are distributed to separate individuals under dual-custody procedures.
documentation_artifacts: Signed attendance log with participant identities and roles
Video recording of key generation sequence
HSM audit log export covering the ceremony window
On-chain transaction hash confirming public key registration
Notarized ceremony summary for FedRAMP package
KEY_CEREMONY.note: This procedure mirrors DNSSEC root key ceremony standards, adapted for SPHINCS+ and HSM deployment.

party_1.holds: One key share — HSM custodied at state government facility

party_2.role: Federal OIG or CMS-designated independent auditor
party_2.holds: One key share — HSM custodied at federal facility

party_3.holds: One key share — custodied at vendor secure facility. Vendor has no access to oracle data.

operating_firm.role: Circuit maintainer and Class C relayer operator
operating_firm.notes: The operating firm does not participate as a keyholder. May observe for technical support under NDA and OIG supervision only. This is a hard architectural constraint, not a policy choice.

rotation_triggers.compromise_detection: Immediate rotation required if any ceremony participant reports key material exposure, HSM tamper event is logged, or anomalous signing activity is detected in audit logs. Rotation must complete within 4 hours of detection.
rotation_triggers.scheduled_rotation: Routine rotation every 12 months minimum. FedRAMP High baseline requires 90-day rotation for signing keys — consult agency ISSO for specific rotation schedule requirements.
rotation_triggers.oracle_source_system_request: If a government oracle source system (e.g., CA-MMIS, CDPH) rotates its own signing infrastructure, the relayer must rotate its verification key registry entry within 48 hours of receiving official notification.

rotation_procedure: New key generated in HSM via abbreviated ceremony (minimum 2 participants).
New public key submitted to on-chain oracle_key_registry via governance transaction.
Transition window: both old and new keys accepted for 24 hours to allow in-flight payloads.
Old key leaf invalidated on-chain after transition window closes.
All proofs generated during transition window are logged with key identifier for auditability.
Rotation event recorded in SIEM with full artifact chain.
KEY_ROTATION.old_key_retention: Old public key retained in key registry history (non-active) for 7 years for proof auditability. Old private key destroyed in HSM after transition window.

authorized_requestors.authorization_mechanism: mTLS client certificate issued by internal PKI. Certificate tied to service account with HSM signing role.
authorized_requestors.scope: May request signatures for oracle_ids it is registered to handle. Cross-oracle signing requests are rejected.

authorized_requestors.authorization_mechanism: HSM partition access via PKCS#11 session with PIN + smart card two-factor.
authorized_requestors.scope: May request signatures for any oracle_id. Requires dual-authorization for bulk signing operations (>100 payloads).

SIGNING_AUTHORIZATION.unauthorized_requestors: All other system components — ZK prover, L2 node, DA layer — have no signing authorization and no HSM access.
request_validation: Requestor identity verified via mTLS certificate chain before HSM session is opened.
Payload hash format validated (32 bytes, BabyBear Poseidon2 parameterization).
Oracle ID checked against authorized oracle registry before signing.
Rate limiting: maximum 1,000 signing requests per minute per service account.

required_fields_per_event.payload_hash: 32-byte BabyBear Poseidon2 hash of signed payload (hex)
required_fields_per_event.signing_key_identifier: On-chain key registry leaf hash identifying which SPHINCS+ key was used
required_fields_per_event.requestor_identity: Service account name + mTLS certificate fingerprint
required_fields_per_event.hsm_sequence_number: Monotonically increasing HSM audit log sequence number — detects log tampering

log_destination: Primary: HSM internal audit log (tamper-evident, FIPS 140-2 Level 3 protected)
Secondary: SIEM (e.g., Splunk, AWS Security Hub) via append-only log stream
Tertiary: CloudTrail or equivalent immutable audit service for FedRAMP evidence package
AUDIT_LOGGING.retention: 7 years minimum (FISMA requirement). Logs are append-only — no deletion or modification permitted.
real_time_alerting: VERIFY_FAIL event → alert within 60 seconds to security operations
KEY_ROTATION event → notify agency ISSO within 1 hour
RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED event → automatic signing suspension + alert
HSM tamper event → immediate signing suspension + incident response
AUDIT_LOGGING.log_integrity: Each log batch is hashed and the hash published to the L2 chain every 15 minutes, creating a cryptographically verifiable audit trail that cannot be retroactively altered.

GOVERNMENT_COMPLIANCE_SUMMARY.FedRAMP: HSM at FIPS 140-2 Level 3, key ceremony documentation, 90-day rotation schedule, append-only SIEM logging, and mTLS service account authorization satisfy FedRAMP Rev5 High baseline cryptographic controls (SC-12, SC-17, IA-3).
GOVERNMENT_COMPLIANCE_SUMMARY.FISMA: Audit logging with 7-year retention, real-time alerting, and immutable log chain satisfy FISMA AU-2, AU-9, and AU-11 controls.
GOVERNMENT_COMPLIANCE_SUMMARY.NIST_SP_800_57: SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s with HSM-protected key material and documented rotation procedures satisfy NIST SP 800-57 Part 1 Rev5 recommendations for post-quantum signing keys.
GOVERNMENT_COMPLIANCE_SUMMARY.NIST_FIPS_205: SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s is a NIST-standardized post-quantum signature scheme (FIPS 205, August 2024). Use of this scheme in a federal procurement context requires no additional cryptographic approval.

O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT.source_entity: County SAWS (58 California county welfare departments)
O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT.authoritative_key_ceremony_principal: DHCS as state Medicaid agency under California WIC §10600 can act as state-level ceremony authority for county SAWS keys, since county welfare is administered under state supervision. DHCS may issue derived signing credentials to county SAWS instances under a state master key ceremony, avoiding 58 separate federal OIG ceremonies.
O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT.county_ceremony_model: RECOMMENDED: DHCS state-level HSM ceremony with county-level sub-key derivation (NIST SP 800-57 key hierarchy). Each county SAWS instance holds a DHCS-endorsed sub-key. The county is the operational key holder; DHCS is the root ceremony principal. This is consistent with California WIC §10600.2 (state supervision of county Medi-Cal functions).
O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT.alternative: 58 separate county ceremonies — legally defensible but operationally impractical for a production deployment timeline.
O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT.legal_basis: California WIC §10600, §10600.2; California Government Code §11549.3; 45 CFR Part 75 does not prohibit state delegation of key authority to sub-grantees operating under state oversight.
O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT.cascade_risk_analysis: The previous recommendation used 'NIST SP 800-57 key hierarchy' / 'sub-key derivation.' In a true key derivation model (e.g., HKDF or BIP32-style HD derivation), the root key material is used to derive child key material mathematically. If the DHCS root key is compromised AND the derivation function and path are known, ALL 58 county sub-keys are simultaneously compromised. This is an unacceptable blast radius — a single DHCS HSM breach compromises every county asset assessment signing capability in California.

endorsement_architecture.key_generation: Each of the 58 county SAWS instances generates its own SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s key pair INSIDE its own county HSM (FIPS 140-2 Level 3 or above). The private key NEVER leaves the county HSM — it is marked non-exportable. The county HSM produces only the public key and signatures.
endorsement_architecture.dhcs_endorsement: DHCS does not derive county keys. DHCS acts as a Certificate Authority: it signs an endorsement certificate binding (county_id, county_public_key, valid_from, valid_thru) using the DHCS root SPHINCS+ key in the key ceremony. This endorsement is stored in OracleKeyRegistry.sol as a second-level entry: the registry contains both the DHCS root certificate and the 58 county endorsements.

security_boundary.dhcs_root_compromise: If the DHCS root key is compromised: DHCS certificates can no longer be trusted. Emergency response: revoke all DHCS-signed certificates via governance transaction. County HSM keys themselves are NOT compromised — they were independently generated and are hardware-bound. Counties can re-enroll under a new DHCS ceremony. Blast radius: DHCS root compromise causes temporary loss of endorsement authority, NOT compromise of county private keys.
security_boundary.county_hsm_compromise: If a single county HSM is compromised: only that county's SAWS signing key is affected. Other 57 counties are unaffected. The compromised county key is revoked in OracleKeyRegistry.sol via county_id governance transaction. The county re-generates a new key pair in a replacement HSM and re-enrolls.

blast_radius_comparison.derivation_model: DHCS root compromise → all 58 counties simultaneously compromised
blast_radius_comparison.endorsement_model: DHCS root compromise → endorsement authority lost, county keys intact; county HSM compromise → 1/58 counties affected

registry_structure.level_1: DHCS root public key — signs county endorsements
registry_structure.level_2: 58 county entries: {county_id, county_sphincs_pubkey, endorsed_by: DHCS, valid_from, valid_thru, revoked: bool}
registry_structure.circuit_verification: Circuit performs TWO Merkle inclusions for O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT: (1) county_public_key ∈ oracle_key_registry (proves county key is enrolled), (2) DHCS_endorsement ∈ dhcs_endorsement_root (proves county key was endorsed by DHCS). Both roots are public inputs. This adds ~40k additional constraints per claim — acceptable.

O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT.nist_reference: NIST SP 800-57 Part 1 Rev. 6 (December 2025) §5.3.4 distinguishes key derivation (child material derived from parent) from key certification (independent keys with cross-certification). The endorsement model follows the certification path. NIST SP 800-57 Part 1 §8.1.4: HSM-bound non-exportable keys are required for asymmetric signing keys in high-assurance environments. California Government Code §11549.3 grants CISO authority to establish key management standards for state systems; county compliance follows under WIC §10600.2 oversight.
O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT._security_fix: v7.5.1-audit Task 2: multi_oracle_ceremony_architecture.O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT updated. Replaced 'sub-key derivation' with 'endorsement model': each county generates independent HSM-bound SPHINCS+ key pair; DHCS acts as CA and endorses county public keys. DHCS root compromise does not cascade to county keys. Blast radius explicitly documented.

O_CLAIMS_HISTORY.source_entity: CA-MMIS Claims History (same system as O1/O6)

federal_ceremony_participant.correct_designation: CMS (Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services) is the correct federal ceremony participant for Medicaid-specific key ceremonies, acting under HHS authority. OIG participates as the audit/oversight witness, not as the operational ceremony principal. The OIG does not administer Medicaid systems — CMS does under 42 CFR Part 430.
federal_ceremony_participant.recommended_structure: Three-party ceremony: (1) DHCS as state Medicaid agency, (2) CMS Region IX (San Francisco) as federal principal, (3) HSM vendor (Entrust, Thales, or AWS CloudHSM) as ceremony hardware custodian. OIG may attend as observer.
federal_ceremony_participant.legal_basis: 42 CFR §430.1 (state plan administration under CMS authority); 45 CFR Part 75 (Uniform Grant Guidance for HHS awards)

multi_oracle_ceremony_architecture._action_required: Legal counsel confirmation of DHCS authority under WIC §10600.2 for county SAWS sub-key delegation. CMS Region IX engagement for federal ceremony participant confirmation. CDPH and SSA separate data sharing agreements for O8.

county_saws_endorsement_architecture.description: Each of the 58 county SAWS instances generates its own SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s key pair inside its own county HSM (FIPS 140-2 Level 3 minimum). The private key never leaves the county HSM — marked non-exportable. DHCS does not derive county keys. DHCS acts as Certificate Authority: it signs an endorsement of (county_id, county_public_key, valid_from, valid_thru) using the DHCS root SPHINCS+ key. County keys are independent hardware-bound key pairs; DHCS endorsement is a certificate, not a derivation.
county_saws_endorsement_architecture.key_generation: County SAWS HSM generates SPHINCS+-SHAKE-128s key pair at enrollment. Public key exported for DHCS endorsement ceremony. Private key: non-exportable, hardware-bound, never leaves county HSM.
county_saws_endorsement_architecture.dhcs_endorsement: DHCS signs endorsement certificate: {county_id, county_sphincs_pubkey, valid_from, valid_thru} using DHCS root key in three-party ceremony (DHCS + CMS Region IX + HSM vendor). Endorsement stored in OracleKeyRegistry.sol two-level structure.

registry_structure.level_1: DHCS root public key — signs county endorsement certificates
registry_structure.level_2: 58 county entries: {county_id, county_sphincs_pubkey, endorsed_by: DHCS, valid_from, valid_thru, revoked: bool}
registry_structure.on_chain: dhcs_endorsement_root public input — Merkle root of all 58 county endorsement certificates

circuit_verification_for_O_ASSET_ASSESSM.constraint_cost: ~40k additional AIR rows per claim (1 additional Merkle inclusion proof, depth 8, 256 county slots)

blast_radius_comparison.derivation_model_DEPRECATED: DHCS root compromise → all 58 county keys simultaneously compromised
blast_radius_comparison.endorsement_model_CURRENT: DHCS root compromise → endorsement authority lost; county HSM keys intact (independently generated, hardware-bound); emergency re-endorsement under new DHCS ceremony, no county HSM operations required. Single county HSM compromise → 1 of 58 counties only.

security_boundary_note.dhcs_root_compromise_response: Revoke all DHCS-signed endorsements via governance transaction. County private keys are NOT compromised — they are independent hardware-bound key pairs. Re-enroll all 58 counties under new DHCS ceremony. Estimated recovery time: 5–15 business days for ceremony + re-enrollment.
security_boundary_note.county_hsm_compromise_response: Revoke that county's key in OracleKeyRegistry.sol via county_id governance transaction. County generates new key pair in replacement HSM. DHCS re-endorses new county public key. Other 57 counties unaffected.

county_saws_endorsement_architecture.nist_reference: NIST SP 800-57 Part 1 Rev. 6 (December 2025) §5.3.4: distinguishes key derivation from key certification. This architecture follows the certification/endorsement path. NIST SP 800-57 Part 1 §8.1.4: asymmetric signing keys in high-assurance environments must be HSM-bound and non-exportable. California Government Code §11549.3: CISO authority over state IT security. California WIC §10600.2: state supervisory authority over county Medi-Cal functions.
county_saws_endorsement_architecture._security_fix: v7.5.2: Replaced NIST SP 800-57 'sub-key derivation' model with endorsement model. DHCS root compromise no longer cascades to county HSM keys. Each county maintains independent hardware-bound key pair. DHCS acts as CA (endorsement), not as root derivation authority. Added dhcs_endorsement_root as new circuit public input. Circuit now requires 2 Merkle proofs for O_ASSET_ASSESSMENT: +40k AIR rows/claim.

ZK_BOUNCER_AUDIT_CIRCUIT.purpose: Verifies cryptographic proof invariants. Executed in-circuit by the ZK Bouncer to audit Rules Engine signatures.

E1_DUPLICATE_DETECTION.description: Verifies that the claim nullifier has not been recorded in the Sparse Merkle Tree (preventing double-claiming).

E7_PROVIDER_VELOCITY_CHECK.description: Verifies that the provider hasn't exceeded the daily volume limit.

E10_NCCI_UNBUNDLING_EDIT.description: Ensures billed codes are not unbundled in-circuit before approval.

E12_GHOST_PATIENT_DEDUPLICATION.description: Fuzzy-hashes DOB + Sex + Soundex of last name inside ZK to stop typo-based patient duplicate billing.
